mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.05) during rate decline periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances where the unemployment rate rises by over 0.5 percentage points.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and real GDP growth indicates robust aggregate demand that supports corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal yields negative 60-day forward returns in over 60% of observed quarters, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the index of industrial production signals weakening manufacturing output that triggers short-term risk-off positioning, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in more than 50% of months following an industrial production decline, or the signal fails to predict direction during recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price inflation combined with a stable or falling unemployment rate creates stagflationary pressure that forces monetary tightening expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined high-inflation and low-unemployment signal yields positive 20-day returns in over 65% of cases, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly housing starts figure exceeding 1.3 million units signals strong residential investment and construction sector momentum that lifts equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of months where housing starts exceed 1.3 million, or the threshold signal shows no predictive power in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls increases household income and consumption capacity, creating immediate positive sentiment that drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of months following positive payroll growth, or the signal Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where industrial production rises while real GDP growth stagnates indicates supply-side bottlenecks that compress profit margins, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO increases while GDPC1 growth < 0.5% quarter-over-quarter
failure_condition: The divergence signal yields positive 20-day returns in over 60% of observed periods, or the margin compression mechanism fails to manifest in earnings revisions data.
